layman_headline: Hyperliquid takes a sharp hit in a fearful market — creating a potential bounce opportunity.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is selling off due to a global tech stock rout spreading into crypto. Hyperliquid, a top decentralized exchange token, has dropped over 12% in a week and 8% just today — much harder than most coins. But its chart still shows a bullish structure with price above key moving averages, suggesting this is a pullback within an uptrend rather than a breakdown. When fear is high and a strong coin drops sharply on broad market panic rather than its own bad news, it often bounces back quickly.
layman_action: Consider a small position in HYPE around $59-60, with a firm stop-loss at $55.50. If it bounces back toward $68, take profits. This is a higher-risk trade in a fearful market, so keep position size modest.
expert_reasoning: HYPE presents a classic 'buy the dip in an uptrend' setup. The chart bias score of +1.1 is the joint-highest among candidates, with price above two major MAs confirming bullish structure. The 12.3% weekly decline has been driven entirely by macro contagion (chip trade unwinding, risk-off in equities spreading to crypto) rather than protocol-specific issues. RSI at 58.69 is neutral — not overbought, providing headroom for recovery. The bullish marubozu candle pattern indicates strong buying conviction even during the selloff. With Fear & Greed at 27 and improving (up from 25 yesterday), contrarian entries in quality assets tend to perform well. The key risk is that macro selling continues, but the defined stop at $55.50 limits downside to ~8% against a 12.7% upside target.
risk_factors: Global risk-off selling from the AI/chip stock rout could intensify and drag crypto lower, especially high-beta alts like HYPE
BTC dominance at 56.1% and rising means capital is rotating away from alts — HYPE could underperform even if BTC stabilizes
CryptoSlate reports 'buyers and bagholders both selling into the rebound below $70K' — suggesting broader crypto distribution that could deepen the correction
watch_list: BTC price action around $62,000-$63,000 support — a breakdown here would likely drag HYPE further down
US equity markets, especially semiconductor stocks — continued AI selloff would maintain risk-off pressure on crypto
HYPE holding above $58 support — a close below this level would invalidate the bullish structure and trigger the stop
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear-driven selloff (F&G 27) triggered by a global chip/AI stock rout spreading into risk assets. BTC has slipped below $63K with high dominance at 56.1%, creating a hostile environment for alts, but fear is improving from 25 to 27 — suggesting the worst of panic selling may be fading.
context_banner: Macro risk-off contagion is pressuring crypto broadly, but sharp pullbacks in structurally bullish assets like HYPE during broad panic historically offer the best swing entry points.

key_signals.description: HYPE RSI at 58.69 is neutral with room to rally, unlike DEXE (85, overbought) — RSI has an 80% win rate in our signal credibility data, making this a high-confidence indicator.
Reports confirm HYPE's -10% drop and ETH falling twice as hard as BTC are driven by the global chip trade unwind — macro contagion, not protocol weakness.
Bitcoin ETFs added $368M in a three-day buying streak, signaling institutional demand persists despite the selloff — a floor for BTC that supports alt recovery.
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) but improving from 25 — contrarian buy signal, though mixed reliability (62% win rate) means we size conservatively.
HYPE chart shows bullish marubozu candle in an uptrend above two MAs with +1.1 bias score — the strongest technical chart setup among all candidates.
